article_title: Raindrop Open-Sources Workshop, a Local MIT-Licensed Debugger That Lets Coding Agents Write and Run Their Own Agent Evals

content_preview.title: Raindrop Open-Sources Workshop, a Local MIT-Licensed Debugger That Lets Coding Agents Write and Run Their Own Agent Evals
content_preview.summary: Raindrop released Workshop, a free local debugger that streams an AI agent's tokens, tool calls, and spans to a browser and lets Claude Code write and fix evals against the trace.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Raindrop, an AI-agent monitoring startup, has released Workshop, which it describes as "an open-source, free, local debugger for AI agents," according to the company's [Raindrop blog](https://www.raindrop.ai/blog/introducing-workshop/). The tool streams every token, tool call, and span an agent emits to a local browser dashboard and, distinctively, lets a coding agent such as Claude Code read those traces and write evaluation tests against them. The project's [GitHub repository](htt...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (752 words) with clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections and appropriate technical depth.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 5 snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0 (GitHub repo) confirms MIT license, the 'local debugger your agent is missing' tagline, port 5899, the ~/.raindrop/raindrop_workshop.db path, 'Live streamed traces', and the full SDK/coding-agent integration lists (CrewAI, LangGraph, OpenCode, Devin, etc.). source-2 (Raindrop blog) confirms 'open-source, free, local debugger for AI agents', 'Debugging an AI agent is miserable', the 0-latency streaming line, 'same SDKs, the same schemas, and the same primitives', and the May 14 launch. source-3 (product page) confirms the 'Claude writes the eval...until every assertion passes' self-healing-loop quote and the install command. source-4 (PRNewswire) confirms the $15M seed, Lightspeed lead, Dec 1 2025 date, and both executive quotes.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (port 5899, db path, version timeline v0.1.6 May 14 → v0.1.15 June 4, $15M) traces to a snapshot. The Koticha quote 'Raindrop is the first monitoring platform to solve this problem' is verbatim. The Bucky Moore quote 'They defined monitoring for AI agents' reproduces the source's 'they defined monitoring for AI agents' verbatim with only the sentence-initial capitalization adjusted (standard practice); meaning is unchanged.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; the Analysis section is clearly labeled and notes the commercial tension (local open-source tool vs. the company's paid cloud product) without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior Raindrop or agent-observability coverage in the archive.
recommendations: Added raindrop.ai to config/source_allowlist.txt as the company's official primary-source domain, resolving the only automated finding.
